title: DIY Art Easel for Kids
summary: An A-frame easel on angled 1x3 legs with two swappable panel faces — a chalkboard on one side, a whiteboard or pinboard on the other — and a dowel tray for holding markers and chalk.
description:
Each side of the A-frame is built from four 1x3 legs, all cut with matching 10° angles so the two faces splay apart identically and meet at a stable, even peak. Build both side frames flat first, then join them at the top with a hinge so the whole easel can fold for storage.

**The two 1/4" panels are what actually determine the art surface** — chalkboard, whiteboard, pinboard, or plain plywood for painting all work in the same frame opening, so pick a panel per side based on what kind of art gets made there. Frame each panel into its side assembly before standing the easel up.

A 3/4" dowel spans the bottom of one side, held by a small ledge, to catch chalk and markers so they don't roll off the tray onto the floor — mount it low enough that a kid can reach it without stretching.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Both ends cut at 10° off square, long point to short point, ends parallel.

steps.title: Build the two side frames
steps.body: Cut the angled legs and build both A-frame sides flat, joining the legs with the cross braces and verticals.

steps.title: Frame in the panels
steps.body: Fit and secure a panel into each side frame's opening.

steps.title: Join the frames at the peak
steps.body: Hinge the two side assemblies together at the top so the easel opens into an A-frame and folds flat for storage.

steps.title: Add the tray
steps.body: Mount the ledge and dowel tray low across the bottom of one side to catch chalk and markers.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly, keeping finish off the panel faces.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: DIY kids art easel with chalkboard and whiteboard panels
